rulings.Case Title: SAEED FAZELL VS. JULIE A MARGETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Notice Of Motion Of Petition For Instructions To 1 Cancel Sales Contract 2 To Retain Non-Refundable Deposit And 3 For Permission To See Recovery Of Any Loss That Might Be Occasioned By A Resale
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 4, 2022 line 1. RECEIVER'S RENOTICE OF MOTION OF PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS TO 1) CANCEL SALES CONTRACT, 2) TO RETAIN NON-REFUNDABLE DEPOSIT, AND 3) FOR PERMISSION TO SEE RECOVERY OF ANY LOSS THAT MIGHT BE OCCASIONED BY A RESALE Hearing Required to address opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DOMINIC MAIONCHI ET AL VS. RUMBLE FITNESS, LLC, A NEW YORK LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Rumble Fitness, Llcs Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Andrew Rebennack, Esq. And Declaration Of Samuel Ray, Esq
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 4, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT RUMBLE FITNESS, LLC'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS is continued to August 10, 2022. No later than August 8, 2022 parties to submit a stipulated order regarding appointment of a discovery referee, if parties reached an agreement. If no agreement was reached parties to each submit names of 3 proposed discovery referees ready and willing to proceed with this matter. No later than noon on August 9, 2022 parties may submit objections to the proposed referees (specific objections only) suggested by the other side or indicate their agreement to a proposed referee. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK O. LEWIS III VS. PAUL E. OEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Limiting Discovery; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Frederick O. Lewis Iii, In The Sum Of $4,155.70
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 4, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANT PAUL OEI'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER LIMITING DISCOVERY; REQUEST FOR ORDER AWARDING MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST FREDERICK O. LEWIS III, IN THE SUM OF $4,155.70 is GRANTED as follows: Defendant to appear for an in-person deposition. Defendant to arrange for a deposition location and deposition officer in San Francisco on August 5 or 6, 2022 (Plaintiff to select date and time and communicate Plaintiff's selection to the Defendant no later than 9:30 a.m. on August 4, 2022). If the cost of the deposition in San Francisco is higher than the cost of the deposition previously set in Tracy, defendant to cover the difference. No sanctions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
